documentation.value: Declares an infrastructure resource of the given type with configuration arguments.
The resource type (e.g., "aws_instance", "azurerm_resource_group").
A local name used to refer to this resource elsewhere in the configuration.
Declares an input variable that can be set when applying the configuration.
The name of the variable, used as var.<name> in expressions.
Declares an output value that is displayed after apply and accessible by other configurations.
The name of the output value.
Declares a data source to fetch information from an external source or another configuration.
The data source type (e.g., "aws_ami", "azurerm_subscription").
A local name for this data source.
Calls a child module with the given source and input variables.
A local name for this module instance.
Declares local values that can simplify expressions and avoid repetition within a module.
Configures Terraform behavior including version constraints, required providers, and backend state storage.
Configures a provider plugin with settings such as region, credentials, and other provider-specific options.
The provider name (e.g., "aws", "azurerm", "google").
Retrieves a value from a map by its key, returning a default value if the key is not found.
The map to look up the value in.
The key to look up.
The default value to return if the key is not found.
Retrieves a single element from a list by its index. The index is zero-based and wraps around using modulo.
The list to retrieve from.
The zero-based index of the element.
Takes an arbitrary number of maps or objects and returns a single map containing a merged set of elements from all arguments.
One or more maps to merge. Later values override earlier ones for duplicate keys.
Combines two or more lists into a single list.
Two or more lists to concatenate in order.
Reads the contents of a file at the given path and returns it as a string.
The path to the file to read, relative to the root module directory.
Reads a file at the given path and renders its content as a template using the supplied variables.
The path to the template file.
A map of template variables to use during rendering.
